Given the code's nature—purely a static interface factory with no external data handling, no secrets, no dynamic execution—this risk score appears unjustified or at least overly conservative.\n\n### Logical assessment:\n\n- The code is a typical autogenerated contract interface wrapper.  \n- No data leaks, no malicious code, no backdoors, no obfuscation.  \n- The purpose is purely to facilitate interaction with a known smart contract.  \n- The minimal security risk score in report 5 does not align with the evidence; it should be 0.\n\n### Final Recommendations:\n\n- **Malware score:** Should remain at 0 across all reports.  \n- **Obfuscated:** Confirmed as 0.  \n- **Risk score:** Given the nature of the code, a risk score of 0 is appropriate; 0.1 is slightly overestimated.\n\n### Overall conclusion:\n\nBased on the detailed analysis, **the code is benign, standard, and free of malicious elements**.
